title: Open-Switch Fault Diagnosis in Dual Three-Phase PMSM Drives Using Domain Consistent Orthogonal Current Trajectory
authors: Yifan Chen, Wenxiang Zhao, Tao Tao, Lei Zhang
abstract: Traditional open-switch fault diagnosis methods largely rely on real-world fault data for model training, leading to high experimental data acquisition and limited fault coverage. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a domain consistent framework for accurate and robust open-switch fault diagnosis in dual three-phase permanent magnet synchronous motor drives. The method analyses the spatial characteristics of six-phase currents with three orthogonal current trajectories under different fault cases. A data unification processing algorithm is developed to correct amplitude and waveform discrepancies between simulated and experimental trajectories, ensuring domain consistent. This consistency enables the diagnostic model to be trained entirely using simulated data while maintaining its applicability in actual experiments. To exploit fault features, a hybrid model is designed for robust classification. The proposed approach can theoretically identify 78 types of open-switch fault scenarios. Experimental validation demonstrates the superior performance of the proposed method.

title: Fiber-optic Current Sensor Based on High order Orbital Angular Momentum Modes Generated by Side-polished Coupler
authors: Lina Xiang, Zhifeng Wang, Heng Guo, Fufei Pang, Jianxiang Wen, Liang Zhang, Heming Wei, Mengshi Zhu, Tingyun Wang
abstract: A polarimetric fiber-optic current sensor utilizing the high order orbital angular momentum (OAM) mode in an air-core fiber (ACF) was demonstrated. The OAM mode is excited through an all-fiber coupler, fabricated by side-polishing and bonding the ACF with a pre-tapered single-mode fiber (SMF). By satisfying the phase-matching condition at 1550 nm, the fundamental SMF mode is efficiently converted into the 7<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-order OAM mode in the ACF with high mode purity. Unlike free-space systems, the integrated all-fiber coupler requires no precise alignment, resulting in a compact and robust assembly. Circular birefringence induced by spin–orbit interaction within the fiber provides high sensitivity to electric current while preserving strong immunity to mechanical vibrations—a robustness that further enhances with increasing mode order. Experimental results show highly linear current sensing with a sensitivity of 0.00126 rad/A, while polarization fluctuations remain within 2.85%, confirming the sensor’s excellent stability under environmental perturbations.

title: Integrated Relative Position and Attitude Control for Multi-Satellite Electromagnetic Formation Flight with Force/Torque-Current Equations
authors: Di Huang, Yuhan Sun, Xujie Yin, Dan Zhao, Haoran Ji
publish: IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Magazine
abstract: This paper investigates the integrated relative position and attitude control of multi-satellite electromagnetic formation flight (EMFF) without reaction wheels, which focuses on solving the coil currents corresponding to electromagnetic forces and torques. By linking the electromagnetic far-field model to the satellite formation dynamics model, a generalized system of force/torque-current equations for multi-satellite EMFF is reconstructed by the inter-satellite electromagnetic interaction model, which is the key to solving the electromagnetic coil currents. Moreover, the Jacobi matrices associated with the system of force/torque-current equations are theoretically analyzed to explore methods for solving the nonlinear equations. Based on this, a homogeneous differential method is proposed to solve the nonlinear equations well. Then, a backstepping position control method and an attitude control law for multi-satellite EMFF are designed respectively. Finally, simulations are used to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title:
Investigation of Quantum Dots Light-Emitting Diodes with Hybrid NiO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Hole Injection Layers
authors: Min-Han Lu, Chih-En Chang, Zheng-Wei Lu, You-Huei Jhang, Jing-Teng Shi, Hsin-Chieh Yu
abstract: In this paper two distinct methods for nickel oxide (NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>on) preparation were utilized to form the hole injection layer (HIL) and systematically compared with the conventional organic material poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene):poly(styrenesulfonate) (PEDOT:PSS) for quantum dots light emitting diodes (QLEDs) fabrication. Green QLED incorporating PEDOT:PSS exhibited a maximum luminance of 549,658 cd/m² and the lowest turn-on voltage of 2.25 V. In contrast, QLED employing NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> nanoparticles (NPs) as the HIL demonstrated a superior current efficiency of 30 cd/A, higher external quantum efficiency (EQE) of 13.19%, and a maximum luminance of 289,349 cd/m². The higher current efficiency observed in the NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> NPs/Poly-TPD hybrid device indicates that an appropriate NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> preparation route combined with a suitable HTL can effectively improve QLED efficiency.

title: On-Device iOS UWB Localization under Angle Dropout: Comparing GPU PF/FGO with Range-Only Estimation
authors: Yoshiyuki Ootani
abstract: Smartphone UWB localization faces a practical angle-dropout condition: angle-of-arrival (AoA) is not always available, which changes the estimation regime. We evaluate iOS on-device localization in two modes—AoA-enabled and range-only—under five noise scenarios with 80 trials each. In AoA-enabled mode, FGO fusion improves UKF by 4.8% (static) and 9.8% (moving), with PF+FGO achieving the best RMSE of 0.116m (static) and 0.163m (moving). In range-only mode, accumulated robust WLS reaches a practical error floor in this setup (0.357 m), leaving only marginal room for UKF (+1.4%) or FGO (+0.2%); under moving targets, FGO provides no meaningful benefit (UKF: −0.2%, PF: +0.1%). Sustained 180 s on-device testing on iPhone 13 Pro confirms all tested major pipelines remain within the 10 Hz real-time budget (P95≤62 ms) with no thermal throttling. These results support mode-aware estimator selection for mobile UWB systems.

title: Inclusion of Inter-crystal Scattering in PET: Analytical Models and Dedicated Reconstruction
authors: Jorge Roser, Hong Phuc Vo, Rebecca Kantorek, Steven Seeger, Magdalena Rafecas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Inter-crystal scattering (ICS) in Positron Emission Tomography (PET) is commonly regarded as a degradation effect that might compromise the image spatial resolution. In parallel, the inclusion of ICS events has also been recognized as a potential approach to increase PET sensitivity, which could be especially beneficial in scenarios where the latter is a limiting factor, such as very small animal imaging. Several methods for the recovery of ICS events have been proposed, many of which aim to locate the first interaction, i.e., the Compton scattering site, usually limited by their success rate, computational burden or data and training dependency. Conversely, this work proposes a physics-based model for ICS events, leading to analytical expressions of the sensitivity image and the system matrix (required by statistical reconstruction algorithms), without the need to identify the original line of response. After validating the model, the work shows how ICS events can be integrated into a joint image reconstruction algorithm (based on list-mode MLEM) together with conventional PET events, for which dedicated analytical models are also developed. To assess the performance of the proposed approach, Monte-Carlo simulated and experimental data of an image quality phantom were obtained with the MERMAID small-fish PET scanner prototype. Both simulation and experimental results indicate that, while slightly decreasing the recovery coefficient values, the inclusion of ICS clearly reduces statistical noise and improves uniformity.

title: Critical Temperature Modulation of Superconducting Ti Films via Anodic Oxidation
authors: Wan Li, Jian Chen, Jinjin Li, Xiaolong Xu, Yanyan Fan, Zhiyou Zhang, Xueshen Wang
abstract: Superconducting transition edge sensors (TESs) have attracted widespread attention owing to their excellent energy resolution performance. The superconducting thin film is the core component of such devices. This study investigated the modulation of the critical temperature (Tc) of Ti films using an anodic oxidation technique. The sample fabrication, anodic oxidation process, and comprehensive characterization are shown. The anodized Ti films show a maximum Tc suppression of nearly 110 mK compared to the non-oxidized reference sample while maintaining excellent electrical stability. Furthermore, the anodized Ti film is etched for different depths to examine the oxidation and superconducting transition processes. The results show that anodic oxidation produces an oxide layer, and the thickness significantly exceeds that attainable by natural oxidation. This robust oxide layer effectively passivates the underlying metallic layer against environmental degradation, contributing to enhanced long-term stability. Our findings demonstrate a feasible, in-situ method for modulating the superconducting properties of Ti films, providing a promising route for protecting optical TESs via anodic oxidation.

title: Leakage-Resilient Multi-Party Signatures for Industrial IoT via Cryptographic Reverse Firewalls
authors: Zengxiang Wang, Yunfan Hu, Zhen Qin, Hu Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The rapid growth of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems has heightened the need for secure cryptographic operations, particularly multi-party digital signatures for decentralized trust. However, existing multi-party signature schemes are vulnerable to insider attacks, and no current solutions address insider-induced data exfiltration effectively. Cryptographic Reverse Firewalls (CRFs) provide a promising solution but face challenges in integration with digital signatures, especially with hash-dependent components. We propose MCRF, a CRF-enhanced multi-party signature scheme designed for IIoT environments. MCRF uses a commitment-based mechanism to optimize the signing process, enabling output-side CRF to re-randomize signatures without compromising correctness. The two-stage CRF architecture-input-side CRF for sanitizing messages and output-side CRF for re-randomizing signatures-ensures efficient protection against both input-triggered and output-stealth exfiltration attacks. MCRF offers strong leakage resistance with minimal computational and communication overhead, making it a scalable solution for secure multi-party signing in IIoT systems.

title: Cooperative Control for Multirobot Systems With Partially Unknown Structural Characteristics: A Game-Theoretic Perspective
authors: Duojicairang Ma, Shuai Li, Long Jin
abstract: Structural characteristics play a pivotal role in the cooperative control of multirobot systems. Although the gravity vector can often be obtained through precalibration, accurately determining the Jacobian, mass, and Coriolis matrices remains challenging because of structural variations and parameter uncertainties, which complicates precise control. This challenge can be reconceptualized as a game of incomplete information, in which each robot, acting as a player, operates with limited knowledge of its structural characteristics. To address this informational deficit, a kinematics- and dynamics-based scheme is proposed to efficiently estimate the Jacobian, mass, and Coriolis matrices of multirobot systems with partially unknown structural characteristics (MRSPUSC) using data-driven techniques. With these key structural matrices continuously estimated, a discrete-time neural dynamics model is then developed to search for the optimal strategy corresponding to the Nash equilibrium of the game. The resulting integrated scheme enables the effective control of MRSPUSC and demonstrates the capability of the proposed approach to overcome challenges arising from partially unknown structural characteristics

title: A Split Narrow-Band Solenoid Magnetic Coupler to Improve Misalignment for 15-kW Underwater Wireless Charging
authors: Amr Mostafa, Haonan Song, Yao Wang, Fei Lu, Hua Zhang
abstract: This article proposes a novel solenoid magnetic coupler structure for underwater charging with two achievements. First, a high-power density of 350.4 kW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is achieved in a saltwater environment. Second, a strong axial misalignment tolerance is achieved, in which a 100-mm-long receiver can maintain 76.1% of the well-aligned power with a 70-mm mismatch. The uniqueness of this design is a split-winding transmitter coupled with a narrow-band receiver with only five turns. High-power underwater experiments are conducted for validation. It achieves maximum power transfer of 15.41 kW with 95.32% DC–DC efficiency in a saltwater environment. When the axial misalignment varies within 70 mm, the output power is above 11.73 kW, and the DC–DC efficiency is maintained higher than 95.27%.

title: Grid-Forming Control Strategy with Improve Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control in Microgrid
authors: Kaizhi Xiong, Long Tao, Pan Yue, Zhenhua Wang, Xiaohan Li, Xuesong Zhou
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Microgrids with grid-connected inverters regulated by the virtual synchronous generator (VSG) suffer from various stability issues as a result of significant variations in the electric power supplied to the load and when those orders about how fast or slow to make the loads go changes. Traditional ones such as the PI control, LADRC, there also some shortcomings: not good enough suppressing overshoots, response times being too long, easily influenced by disturbances from noises. The article fixed these problems with its enhanced version of the ILADRC system that has added RSDF along with a cascaded LEOSO-DC. It is based on the VSG-based secondary frequency regulation framework and is equipped with an extra third-order linear extended-state-observer (LESO) that incorporates an additional uncontrolled state vector. In addition to providing us an extra degree-of-freedom for such a larger model our new observer can now do what we ask it to; estimate both the current disturbance and the accumulated one at the same time which is really good news when you're trying to get rid of noise across every single possible band. Also transfer functions for VSG control loops is acquired for analysis proof of enhanced disturbance rejections as well as sturdy properties supplied via proposed scheme. And then we do the digital simulation part, showing whether our approach can actually handle dynamic stuff inside microgrids.

title: Transient and Permanent Defect Analysis in AlGaN/GaN P-GaN Gate HEMTs Under Cumulative DC Voltage Stress
authors: Christale Crouard, Jonathan Godillon, Jean-François Pieprzyk, Patrick Tounsi
abstract: This study investigates the reliability of 650 V AlGaN/GaN HEMTs using a novel Cumulative Step Stress method with incrementally increasing DC voltage steps. By monitoring transient signal time constants and final leakage levels, both newly induced and accumulated defects are tracked. Time-domain analysis reveals field-dependent transient leakage currents associated with trap states and parasitic capacitances, with permanent degradation observed above 1000 V. Frequency-domain analysis identifies defect-related noise peaks and bias-dependent frequency shifts, indicating damage onset above 900 V.

title: Hydrogen-Mediated Charge Compensation PBTI Degradation in ALPO with IGZO Channel Devices
authors: Atul Sachan, Sandip Mondal
abstract: Hydrogen-driven charge compensation mechanism unlocks exceptional bias temperature instability (BTI) and selfhealing capability in low-temperature processed oxide TFTs, establishing ALPO as a dual-function dielectric that simultaneously neutralizes traps and regenerates interface quality. The devices exhibit excellent channel control, with transconductance improving from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.1 \mu \mathrm{S}/\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> at 25° C to 13.5 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{S}/\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> at 105° C. Under positive bias stress (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Vgstress}=+10 \mathrm{V}$</tex> to +18 V), threshold voltage shifts (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{T}}$</tex>) follow a sub-linear power law (slope <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.21-0.28$</tex>), confirming field-assisted, dispersive trapping at the ALPO/IGZO interface. Beyond <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \Delta V_{T}$</tex> polarity reversal reveals thermally activated hydrogen migration from ALPO that passivates deep traps and forms shallow donors, marking a self-compensating defect-healing mechanism for nextgeneration reliable, flexible electronics.

title: Experimental Characterization of Electromigration-Induced Stress Evolution in a 22nm-Technology
authors: S. Rothe, J. Lienig, S. Muniswamy, M. Herklotz
abstract: Electromigration research has experienced a paradigm shift within the last years, moving from conventional current-density verification to physics-based modeling to measure for EM robustness. However, the new models lack practical validation and established methods to experimentally characterize the modeling parameters. In this work, we analyze existing characterization schemes and propose a novel and simplified approach to experimentally obtain the EM parameters for physics-based modeling. We validate both our new method and a previously published interpolation method with experimental lifetime data for 80 nm-pitch <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Cu}(\text{Mn})$</tex> metallization using GlobalFoundries 22FDX® FDSOI (fully depleted silicon-on-insulator) technology.

title: Short-Circuit Current Suppression Strategy Based on Electrical Distance Partitioning and Hierarchical Topology Optimization
authors: Yihong Zhu, Xin Shan, Yi Wang, Ji Zhang, Qi Li, Sicheng Huang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: To address the increasingly severe problem of excessive short-circuit currents (SCC) in large-scale interconnected power grids and the limitations of existing topology optimization methods-specifically their lack of a global perspective and high computational complexity-this paper proposes a hierarchical SCC suppression strategy based on electrical distance partitioning. First, the Shortest Path Faster Algorithm (SPFA) is employed to quantify the electrical coupling between nodes, establishing a “Strong Coupling Aggregation” criterion to partition the grid into structurally coherent sub-areas. Subsequently, a bi-level optimization framework is constructed: the upper level implements “Inter-area Decoupling” to reconstruct global impedance distribution, while the lower level performs “Intra-area Fine-tuning” to eliminate local violations under strict connectivity constraints. Case studies on the IEEE 39bus system demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces the maximum SCC from 51.55 p.u. to 36.86 p.u., achieving global control of fault levels. Furthermore, comparative analysis with an exact Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) benchmark verifies that the proposed heuristic strategy attains global optimality in terms of operation cost (4 switching actions) while improving computational efficiency by approximately 28 times compared to the Gurobi solver. This validates the strategy as a highly efficient and reliable solution for real-time decisionmaking in complex power grids.

title: Spacer Trapping Effect on Hot Carriers Degradation Dynamics for Advanced FDSOI Nodes
authors: T. Mota Frutuoso, W. Vandendaele, J. Mayolet, B. Mohamed, J. Kanyandekwe, L. Laraignou, M. Dartois, F. Bringuier, V. Lapras, V. Jousseaume, C. Guerin, A. Raison, K. Romanjek, Z. Chalupa, M. C. Cyrille, C. Fenouillet-Beranger, B. Duriez, X. Garros
abstract: This work investigates the impact of spacer charge trapping on hot-carrier degradation in 10 nm FDSOI transistors using low-k SiCO spacers. Two SiCO variants-high- and low-trapping-are compared through hot-carrier stress experiments and CET map analysis. Results show that spacer-induced degradation dominates early stress but saturates quickly. Devices with underlapped junctions exhibit stronger spacer-related degradation, while optimized low-trapping spacers completely suppress this effect. Time-to-Failure analysis was performed for each variant and shows that traps do have an impact on device lifetime but is strongly dependent on the degradation criterion.

title: Drone-Augmented Ground Sensors: A Model-Based Systems Engineering Approach to Area Denial
authors: Benedetto Fusco, Ashley Hubert, Dan Velarde, Kathryn Wegler, Justus Whitaker, Vikram Mittal
abstract: Conventional minefields, while historically effective, present significant operational, humanitarian, and legal challenges due to their cost, rigidity, and indiscriminate nature. Modern military operations require area-denial solutions that are precise, adaptive, and ethically compliant, minimizing risk to civilians while maintaining tactical effectiveness. This research presents a Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) approach to designing a non-lethal, sensor-augmented area denial system capable of detecting, classifying, and reporting adversary movements without the use of explosives. The design process begins with a comprehensive stakeholder analysis integrating operational, policy, and humanitarian perspectives to define system requirements and guide architectural decisions. A formal Analysis of Alternatives evaluates multiple concepts against criteria such as effectiveness, scalability, cost, reusability, and disposability, providing a quantitative basis for selecting the optimal configuration. The resulting design integrates a network of low-power, distributed ground sensors with autonomous drone overwatch to enable persistent monitoring, positive identification, and human-in-the-loop verification for engagement decisions. The system supports rapid deployment, secure data transmission, autonomous target classification, and safe end-of-life behaviors in compliance with international standards. By leveraging MBSE, the project ensures traceability from stakeholder needs to logical and physical architectures, supporting future adaptability and scalability. The proposed Drone Hub with Puck Sensors concept demonstrates how hybrid sensor-drone systems can achieve tactical area denial while mitigating the long-term hazards associated with traditional minefields. This work provides a framework for the development of future non-lethal, ethically responsible area denial systems that balance operational effectiveness, humanitarian considerations, and technological innovation.

title: A Data Framework for Behavioral Health with Digitized Drawings and Photographs
authors: F Mohamed Abdullah, Jihye Kwon, Bagwan, Ishaan Gupta, David Moxley, Pradeeban Kathiravelu
abstract: Multiple data sources store various modalities for analyzing behavioral health. Beyond clinical frameworks generating healthcare data, individuals with mental illnesses create drawings and photographs that can reveal insights into their journey toward recovery. This paper describes a data framework that complements traditional healthcare data with patient-generated artistic representations, enabling patients to curate and share them with their healthcare providers.We evaluate the state of the art in research and clinical practices for using patient-generated health data (PGHD) in behavioral healthcare. We then examine how integrated care approaches can empower patients to achieve a better recovery trajectory. We propose and build Beehive as a data framework for patients to store digitized drawings and photographs, along with captions that detail the narrative of each image. These figures are then shared directly with the behavioral health provider through Beehive, complementing traditional means of behavioral and mental healthcare. Our evaluations of performance and functionality during deployment showed promising results for Beehive. We aim to deploy Beehive in two Anchorage-based community health organizations.We see our approach as the first step toward providing integrated, patient-centric mental healthcare with dignity. We also observe the potential to apply our framework to prior work advocating integrated behavioral health approaches.

title: Combined Quantum and Post-Quantum Security Performance Under Finite Keys
authors: Aman Gupta, Ravi Singh Adhikari, Anju Rani, Xiaoyu Ai, Robert Malaney
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Recent advances in quantum-secure communication have highlighted the value of hybrid schemes that combine Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) with Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC). Yet most existing hybrid designs omit realistic finite-key effects on QKD key rates and do not specify how to maintain security when both QKD and PQC primitives leak information through side-channels. These gaps limit the applicability of hybrid systems in practical, deployed networks. In this work, we advance a recently proposed hybrid QKD-PQC system by integrating tight finite-key security to the QKD primitive and improving the design for better scalability. This hybrid system employs an information-theoretically secure instruction sequence that determines the configurations of different primitives and thus ensures message confidentiality even when both the QKD and the PQC primitives are compromised. The novelty in our work lies in the implementation of the tightest finite-key security to date for the BBM92 protocol and the design improvements in the primitives of the hybrid system that ensure the processing time scales linearly with the size of secret instructions.

title: Long-Short Step Coordinated Photovoltaic Power Generation Fusion Forecasting
authors: Jiayang Weng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power generation forecasting is crucial for the stability and scheduling efficiency of the smart grid. However, the high volatility and non-stationarity of solar irradiance lead to continuous error accumulation when predicting over longer time horizons, posing a significant challenge for long-term forecasting. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel hybrid framework integrating iTransformer, Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network. Specifically, the iTransformer is employed to capture global temporal dependencies. Internally, a gating mechanism replaces the traditional feedforward network with a MoE layer, enabling dynamic adaptation to diverse weather patterns (e.g., sunny vs. cloudy days). Subsequently, a BiLSTM layer is utilized to optimize local temporal features and smooth the predicted sequence. Furthermore, to enhance the stability of longterm forecasting, we introduce a Long-Short Timestep Collaborative Forecasting Strategy. This strategy corrects the training loss by calculating the high-precision residuals of smallscale temporal predictions, thereby guiding the optimization process of long-term forecasts and mitigating performance degradation caused by long sequences. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces forecasting errors under different weather conditions and exhibits superior forecasting performance in terms of both accuracy and stability.

title: On the Comprehensive Smart Irrigation Modeling and Experimental Validation
authors: Md Zunaid Hossen, Sajal Kumar Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) integrate with CyberPhysical Systems (CPS), such as smart irrigation, which open new possibilities for irrigation management though have significant challenges like cyber security issues, integrity of real time data. This paper presents a comprehensive mathematical model of a cyber-physical smart irrigation system designed for cyber threat analysis and resilient to cyber-attacks. The proposed model acts as the foundation for developing a model-based attack detection and mitigation strategy. The mathematical model addresses significant research gaps in Cyber-physical smart irrigation for attack resilient water flow control and management. The proposed mathematical model is validated through experimentation and simulation.

title: Enhanced Multi-Sensor IoT Architecture for Real-Time Gas Leakage Detection, Automated Mitigation, and SMS Alerts
authors: Noshin Un Noor, Mohammad Anwar Hossain, Md. Minhajul Islam, Md Shahadat Hossain Shishir, Md. Shahariar Sarkar, Kh. Mustafizur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON)
abstract: The gas leaking and the risk of fire are not only a dangerous threat to safety, but also to our natural environment. Conventional detection systems do not provide real-time monitoring, remote access and automatic response. This paper presents an IoT based gas leakage system with multiple sensors for burring low accuracy and more efficiency. By using SnO2 gas sensors (MQ-3, MQ-4, and MQ-7) with the Arduino and NodeMCU (ESP8266) detects harmful levels of gas on a constant level. When gas is sensed, it triggers alarm in the form of buzzer and exhaust fan, sends SMS via GSM, summarizes data and stores it on the cloud for easy access for remote checking. Validation studies show the system has a robust detection rate of 95%, and is a dependable, real-time risk indicator. The system is also scalable, cost-effective, and can be deployed for safety in industrial as well as residential safety, the results achieves better early response and preventive maintenance through IoT implementation..

title: Bad Data Detection Methods for Phasor Measurement Units
authors: Ziliang Huang, Yicong Chen, Yao Liu, Jun Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: The integration of Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) into modern power grid infrastructure has revolutionized wide-area monitoring and control systems by providing synchronized, high-resolution measurements of voltage and current phasors. However, the accuracy and reliability of PMU measurements are critically challenged by various types of bad data, including random measurement errors, systematic biases, cyber-attacks such as false data injection (FDI) and GPS spoofing attacks, communication failures, and hardware malfunctions. Through comparative analysis of detection performance metrics including precision, recall, F1-score, detection rate, and false alarm rate across various attack scenarios and system configurations, we evaluate the strengths and limitations of each approach. Our findings indicate that hybrid deep learning approaches, particularly the combination of CNN and LSTM architectures, achieve superior performance with F1-scores exceeding 96% for anomaly detection, while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-time applications.

title: Adaptive Multi-Objective Optimization Design of Rotary Magnetorheological Damper
authors: Junqiang Li, Xiaoqi Lu, Juan Wang, Dong Yang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Aiming at the lightweight design requirements for the rotor structure of the rotary magnetorheological damper (RMED), an optimization design method based on optiSlang is proposed. A rotary magnetorheological damper with an L-shaped rotor cross-section was designed, and its magnetic circuit was completed. The finite element method was applied to establish a finite element model of the damper, obtaining the distribution of magnetic flux lines inside the damper and the magnetic field intensity distribution in the working area. On this basis, with the minimum moment of inertia of the damper as the optimization objective and the required torque of the damper as the constraint, the main structural parameters of the rotor were selected as the optimization design variables. The multi-objective optimization method of optiSlang was employed to obtain the optimal structural dimensions of the damper rotor. The research results indicate that the proposed method can achieve the optimization design targeting the minimum rotor inertia of the damper, and the findings are of significant importance for the practical application of rotary magnetorheological dampers.

title: Exploratory Analysis of Dementia Risk Factors Using Distributed Data Systems
authors: Taryn Cail, Peash Ranjan Saha
abstract: Dementia is one of the leading diseases that causes disability among the elderly. Usually, dementia is caused by a combination of demographic, educational, lifestyle, and medical factors. An analysis of these risk factors is crucial for identifying priority areas for intervention to reduce dementia cases globally. Some analysis has been performed in the past to identify common risk factors for dementia based on data sources with a uniform structure. However, the evolution of different computing platforms enforces the accumulation of data in multiple modes. Moreover, the sources of the datasets may not be in proximity. Accommodating such modern paradigms of data in dementia risk factor analysis is paramount. In this research, we present a distributed data system which is capable of integrating multiple publicly available data sources and demonstrating the relationship between lifestyle and dementia. The data sources can be in different proximity and have semi-structured data with different schemas, which includes individuals demographic and lifestyle information. Our proposed distributed data system efficiently handles the semi-structured data files (e.g., CSV or JSON) and produces a correlation analysis. The analysis focused on descriptive statistics rather than predictive modeling, highlighting modest associations between dementia and several factors such as education, smoking, and alcohol consumption. We implemented the system in the Hadoop distributed database framework (HDFS) with an integration of PySpark and MongoDB, and experimented with two large public data sets from Kaggle. The results suggest that higher educational attainment may offer a protective effect, while lifestyle shows moderate associations. Our proposed system is scalable, and preliminary experiments demonstrate its ability to support a reproducible exploratory and descriptive analysis of risk factors for dementia.

title: An Agentic, Multimodal RAG Framework for Risk-Aware Surgical Robotics User Manual Creation
authors: Paige Kendell, Alexander Semenov, Wei Zhang
abstract: In the medical device domain, the user manual is a safety-critical and foundational artifact that directly impacts clinical outcomes. For complex surgical robotic systems, inaccurate manuals increase procedural risks. Despite their importance, traditional user manual creation is labor-intensive, error-prone, and typically deferred until late in development, limiting early validation and increasing project risk. This paper presents an AI-driven framework for accelerated, risk-aware user manual creation using agentic AI and video-based content ingestion. The proposed system combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with autonomous AI agents to continuously draft, review, and refine manual content throughout the product lifecycle. By integrating surgical workflow videos with design and regulatory documentation, the framework enables richer contextual understanding and more precise procedural guidance. Practical insights from real-world surgical robotics development demonstrate how early, automated documentation can reduce human error, accelerate development timelines, and strengthen clinical safety, transforming user manuals from a compliance requirement into a proactive risk-mitigation tool. The paper will present: 1) Introduction: motivation, problem statement and our objective, 2) Regulatory and clinical context, 3) Technical architecture of the AI-driven system, 4) Methods for video-based content ingestion and semantic reasoning, 5) Implementation results and lessons learned from real-world surgical robotics development, and 6) Conclusion and future work.

title: Determination of the Lower Dead Time Threshold for the Onset of Bipolar Degradation in SiC MOSFETs
authors: C. Herrmann, J. Prenzel, T. Basler
abstract: This paper studies the occurrence of Bipolar Degradation in 1200 V Silicon Carbide Power MOSFETs in dependence on the dead time duration. While previous work focused on the general influence of dead time duration on bipolar degradation, this work seeks a lower threshold value, below which stacking fault growth can be significantly reduced or entirely avoided. To the author's knowledge, this is the first time, a dead time test down to 50 ns has been conducted using special devices for getting statistical evidence. Additionally, the impact of bipolar degradation on device parameters such as output characteristic and leakage currents has been investigated.

title: Variability and Retention Investigation for Ferroelectric FET with Pseudo-MFMIS Structure
authors: Ryo Ono, Kensuke Ota, Yusuke Shuto, Jun Okuno, Tsubasa Yonai, Masaki Sakakibara, Maximilian Lederer, Peter Reinig, Konrad Seidel, Ruben Alcala, Uwe Schroeder, Thomas Mikolajick, Akihiko Kato, Taku Umebayashi, Kentaro Akiyama
abstract: We systematically studied device-to-device variability and retention in pseudo metal-ferroelectric-metal-insulator-semiconductor (MFMIS) based ferroelectric memory. Threshold voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{V}_{\text {th }}$</tex>) variability is found to increase with shrinking MFM capacitor area, which is consistent with the random polarization switching in polycrystalline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Hf}_{1-\mathrm{x}} \text{Zr}_{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex>. In contrast, the variability can be suppressed by scaling down the total transistor channel area, revealing a universal dependence of the variability on the MFM-to-transistor area ratio. Retention characteristics were measured with various ferroelectric and gate-oxide thicknesses. Fast memory window degradation was observed for thinner HZO or thinner gate oxides, indicating that charge loss through these dielectric layers, rather than intrinsic ferroelectric depolarization, dominates retention characteristics. Furthermore, devices programmed with a reduced memory window exhibit markedly improved retention, even with thinner dielectrics. These results establish comprehensive understanding for variability and retention, providing insight into the design methodologies for ferroelectric memories and computing-inmemory applications.

title: Harnessing Entanglement in Quantum Federated Learning Under Efficiency Constraints
authors: Shiva Raj Pokhrel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We introduce an entanglement-assisted framework for Quantum Federated Learning (QFL) under local operations classical communication (LOCC), and limited shared entanglement. Unlike prior work assuming unlimited resources, we focus on efficient protocols with a feasible number of samples. We quantify how much entanglement can be extracted for learning and how much is needed for synchronization, showing that extraction is cheaper than global synchronization and that stateaware protocols offer no worst-case advantage. Our results provide actionable guidelines for designing efficient, resourceaware QFL systems.
